name: Healthcare MCP Server
what_it_does: Healthcare MCP server from Integrail enabling AI agents to interact with healthcare data systems — querying patient records, retrieving clinical data via FHIR API, accessing medical coding (ICD, CPT, SNOMED), and integrating healthcare information into agent-driven clinical decision support and medical data workflows.
use_cases: Querying patient records via FHIR from clinical decision support agents
Retrieving medical coding information from coding assistance agents
Accessing lab results and medication lists from clinical review agents
Building HIPAA-compliant medical data pipelines from health IT agents
Integrating EHR data with AI reasoning from clinical AI agents
Medical research data access from clinical research agents
not_for: Non-healthcare applications (specialized and complex for non-medical use)
Consumer health apps without proper HIPAA compliance infrastructure
Teams without proper healthcare data compliance frameworks
best_when: A healthcare IT team needs AI agents to access clinical data via standard healthcare APIs (FHIR) — with appropriate HIPAA compliance infrastructure in place.
avoid_when: You don't work in healthcare or lack proper HIPAA compliance — healthcare data is highly regulated and requires significant compliance infrastructure.

auth.notes: FHIR server authentication varies by EHR (SMART on FHIR OAuth2, API keys). Configuration depends on connected EHR system. Proper HIPAA BAA required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public MCP from Integrail — free for approved healthcare use cases
pricing.paid_tiers: Contact Integrail for enterprise healthcare integrations
pricing.notes: Public healthcare MCP from Integrail (healthcare AI company). Free public version. Enterprise healthcare data contracts required for production use.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on connected EHR system rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA BAA required before handling real patient data — critical compliance requirement
FHIR authentication varies by EHR system — complex multi-system auth setup
PHI (Protected Health Information) handling requires strict data governance
From Integrail (commercial healthcare AI company) — verify licensing for production
FHIR API versions (R4, R5) vary by EHR system
Audit logging of all data access may be required by compliance
agent_readiness.security_notes: CRITICAL: HIPAA compliance required. PHI must be handled with appropriate safeguards. HTTPS mandatory. Audit logging required. HIPAA BAA required with all data processors.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: HIPAA. PHI requires BAA. HTTPS. Audit logging. FHIR OAuth scoping.
